signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical Shock + Carry + Vol
signals.text: USD/JPY at 160.31 — the pair holds above the 160.00 threshold despite a US-Iran peace agreement and a -4.87% crash in crude oil. While the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz triggers a risk-on equity rally (Nikkei +4.99%), the 300bp Fed-BoJ carry advantage (Fed 3.75% vs BoJ 0.75%) sustains the bid. JPY COT LF net short -115,036 remains crowded, and the 25d RR at -1.20 indicates the options market is paying for downside protection against BoJ intervention risk, which rises on a sustained break above 160. Carry dominates the flow until VIX > 25.

signals.title: USD/CAD — Retail Divergence + Correlation Break
signals.text: USD/CAD at 1.3993 — retail sentiment is 92% short (extreme), diverging from COT LF net short -60,671 (institutional short). This retail-institutional alignment on the short side is fractured by a significant correlation break: USD/CAD vs WTI Oil corr at -0.231 (z=-1.62σ) vs the norm of -0.019. The 150bp Fed-BoC carry advantage (Fed 3.75% vs BoC 2.25%) and positive USD CESI (+22) support the topside, suggesting retail shorts face capitulation risk if the pair tests 1.4000.

signals.title: AUD/USD — CB Stance + Vol Premium + COT
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7073 — RBA 4.35% (HIKING) vs Fed 3.75% (ON HOLD) provides a -60bp carry premium favoring AUD. Despite the hawkish CB stance and COT LF net long +38,092 (68.8% long), the options market remains cautious with a 25d RR of -0.80 (put skew). AUD/USD IV at 14.8% vs HV30 at 8.2% creates a +6.5% VRP, the second highest in G10, signaling that the market is pricing in significant volatility ahead of the RBA meeting on 16 Jun.

signals.title: NZD/USD — Positioning + CESI Divergence
signals.text: NZD/USD at 0.5823 — COT LF net short -22,354 (specs short) aligns with a negative NZD CESI (-4 misses), confirming a fundamental weakness bias. The RBNZ real policy rate of -0.83% (accommodative) vs the USD real rate of +1.31% (restrictive) creates a structural headwind for the pair. Retail sentiment is 83% long, positioning for a reversal that lacks institutional or data support. Watch for a break of 0.5800 to accelerate spec shorting.
